title: senna artemisioides nothosubsp. coriacea
intro: Senna artemisioides nothosubsp. coriacea, also known as silver cassia, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. The plant is a shrub native to Australia, and its leaves and pods have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include laxative, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Senna artemisioides nothosubsp. coriacea include its ability to treat constipation, diarrhea, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and it can also be used as a component in herbal blends or as a dried leaf or pod in herbal remedies.
